compiled_state.title: RED pytest: assert implementation-plan SKILL.md contains REPLAN_ESCALATE emission logic
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Story ca76-bb4e: RED pytest task. Create a structural test that asserts implementation-plan/SKILL.md contains the REPLAN_ESCALATE emission logic. This test FAILS against the unmodified SKILL.md (RED) and PASSES after Task 4 adds the logic.

## TDD Requirement (RED)
Create `tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py` with tests that:
1. Assert SKILL.md contains 'REPLAN_ESCALATE' text
2. Assert SKILL.md contains 'REPLAN_ESCALATE: brainstorm' signal format
3. Assert SKILL.md contains 'EXPLANATION' as a signal payload field
4. Assert the Ambiguity Scan section contains logic for when to emit the signal (condition: SC cannot be satisfied / contradicted by codebase state)
5. Assert SKILL.md distinguishes REPLAN_ESCALATE from STATUS:blocked (the ambiguity distinction)

All tests MUST fail against the current unmodified SKILL.md. Run `poetry run pytest tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py -x` to confirm RED before marking complete.

Also update `.test-index` to add the RED marker:
```
plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md:tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py [test_implementation_plan_emits_replan_escalate_signal]
```
This is a new line — do NOT modify existing lines for this source file.

Read `plugins/dso/docs/contracts/replan-escalate-signal.md` first to understand the exact format to assert for.

## Files to Create/Modify
- `tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py` (new)
- `.test-index` (add RED marker line)

## Acceptance Criteria

### AC1: Test file exists
```
Verify: test -f tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py && echo PASS
```

### AC2: Tests are RED against unmodified SKILL.md (must fail before implementation)
```
Verify: poetry run pytest tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py -x -q 2>&1 | grep -q 'FAILED\|AssertionError' && echo PASS
```

### AC3: .test-index contains RED marker for SKILL.md → test file
```
Verify: grep 'test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py.*\[test_implementation_plan_emits_replan_escalate_signal\]' .test-index && echo PASS
```

### AC4: Test file asserts REPLAN_ESCALATE signal presence
```
Verify: grep -q 'REPLAN_ESCALATE' tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py && echo PASS
```

### AC5: Test file has at least 3 distinct test functions
```
Verify: grep -c 'def test_' tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py | awk '$1>=3{print "PASS"}' 
```

comments.body:
GAP_ANALYSIS_AMENDMENT: Canonical string requirement.

Assertions in the pytest must match the exact canonical strings defined in the contract document (fa7b-d4e9). Specifically:
- Assert 'REPLAN_ESCALATE: brainstorm EXPLANATION:' (with the exact spacing and colon) as the signal prefix
- Do NOT assert section headings like 'Unsatisfiable Criteria Detection' specifically — assert the signal format string itself and surrounding condition logic

This prevents the RED→GREEN transition failing due to string mismatch between what bd1a-14a3 tests and what 43d6-4603 implements.

AC5 addition: assertions use canonical signal prefix 'REPLAN_ESCALATE: brainstorm EXPLANATION:' from contract doc fa7b-d4e9.
Verify: grep 'REPLAN_ESCALATE: brainstorm EXPLANATION:' tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py && echo PASS

Source: Gap analysis finding 2 (implicit_assumption: canonical string alignment)
comments.author: Test
